narrative: Your law school application was denied because our predictive model, which evaluates the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try based on academic and background factors, estimated a 53.4% chance that you would not pass. This prediction tips the scale just over the threshold we use for admissions decisions, leading to the rejection to help ensure strong bar passage rates for the program. The model's assessment focused heavily on your Year 3 school decile ranking, which was in the bottom tenth, strongly pushing the prediction toward expecting a bar failure—far below the mid-level rankings typical of students who passed. This was the single biggest factor influencing the outcome for you. On a more positive note, your undergraduate GPA of 3.40 stood out as a helpful element, pulling the prediction back toward success since it exceeds the average of 3.24 among those who passed the bar. Your upper-middle family income bracket also contributed favorably, aligning well with the majority of successful bar takers and nudging the model slightly in your favor. While your LSAT score of 26 was notably lower than the 37.27 average for passers, and your Year 1 decile was similarly low at the first tenth compared to their sixth-decile average, these played supporting roles rather than leading ones. Being a full-time student matched the norm for 93% of those who passed, so it didn't sway things much either. Overall, your profile showed strengths like your solid undergrad performance that echoed successful applicants, but the low Year 3 ranking overshadowed them, placing you outside the typical accepted range where consistent mid-to-high deciles dominate. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score closer to the passing average and strengthening your later academic deciles through targeted coursework or post-bac programs that demonstrate upward trends. Many students in similar spots have succeeded by addressing these gaps—reach out to admissions advisors for personalized strategies or appeal options if new achievements can update your file. With your promising UGPA as a foundation, focusing on these steps could shift future predictions in your favor.
